intent.symptom_summary: Tab bar UI does not match design/reference.html across 8 structural/visual points (method chip, active-tab accent, dirty-XOR-close, tabbar geometry/separators, SVG close icon, +/spacer/chevron actions, tab padding/gap, double bottom border). Feature 004 shipped 'look only' with zero visual-fidelity ACs; close all drift as new feature 005.
intent.desired_summary: Target = reference's 8-item spec with TIERED fidelity (not flat pixel-perfect): (1) Verification = computed-style EXACT equality on enumerated props (font-size, padding, gap, border, color-tokens) PLUS screenshot diff with explicit anti-aliasing pixel-threshold; 'verified' = computed-style assertions pass, not eyeballed. (2) Semantic/interaction items EXACT + non-negotiable: active ::before/::after, dirty-dot-XOR-close, method chip. (3) 'More tabs' chevron = VISUAL fidelity only; overflow behavior OUT — no working overflow required to pass. (4) HEAD method color is closed: add --m-head/.method.HEAD token (colored HEAD); uncolored HEAD NOT accepted. (5) Geometry items exact-by-computed-style. Hard constraint: opt-in contract stays backward-compatible — non-closable render path byte-identical, proven by test.
intent.verbatim_prompt: # Tab bar — full visual fidelity to `design/reference.html` > Intake note for `/research` — feature `005-tab-bar-visual-fidelity` (follow-on to shipped 004). ## Context Feature 004 (working tabs state machine) shipped and is **spec-complete** — but its spec declared `design/reference.html` "look only" (`spec.md:13,107`) with **zero visual-fidelity ACs**. A design-auditor run surfaced large visual drift. After scope cross-check, the user chose to close **all** drift, including new structural elements (method chips, overflow chevron, dirty-dot-replaces-close). This is **not a `/fix` on 004**: it changes the exported `TabDescriptor` contract on the 002 Tabs primitive and adds a new closable interaction model. Correct framing = a **new feature `005-tab-bar-visual-fidelity`** routed through `/specify → /plan → /breakdown → /implement`, with the contract change recorded in 002 lineage (the AC-29 pattern 004 already used). ## What the reference requires (verified vs current impl) | # | Target (reference) | Current | Files | |---|---|---|---| | 1 | `.method` color chip inside each tab before label | none — `deriveLabel` emits string only | `Tabs.tsx`, `TabBar.tsx:73` | | 2 | Active = top accent bar (`::before`) + bottom mask (`::after`) + `--bg` bg | bottom underline `inset 0 -2px accent` + `--accent-soft` wash | `Tabs.css:142-148` | | 3 | Dirty dot **XOR** close button (dot is clickable-to-close) | badge `●` alongside + always-present hover-reveal ✕ | `Tabs.tsx`, `TabBar.tsx:73` | | 4 | `.tabbar` `--bg-sunken`, 36px, `border-bottom`, `padding-right:8px`; per-tab `border-right` | `--bg`, ~32px, no separators | `TabBar.css` | | 5 | Close = 16px SVG `x` (Icon size 11), always visible | unicode `✕`, `opacity:0` hover-reveal | `Tabs.tsx`, `Tabs.css:295-337` | | 6 | `+` (Icon plus 13) inline + `flex:1` spacer + "More tabs" chevron (Icon chevronDown 13) | single `+` text in actions slot | `TabBar.tsx`, `TabBar.css` | | 7 | Tab padding `0 10px 0 12px`, gap 8px, label `flex:1` ellipsis | symmetric `0 0.75rem`, gap 6px | `Tabs.css:83-84` | | 8 | Single bottom border | double (`Shell.css:130` + `Tabs.css:38`) | both | Reuse assets confirmed present: `Icon` atom (`atoms/Icon.tsx`, has `x`/`plus`/`chevronDown`); `.method.*` per-method color classes in `tokens.css` (resolve at runtime via `data-mstyle` on ``). Gaps found: base `.method { font-family: var(--font-mono) }` rule missing; no `--m-head`/`.method.HEAD` token. ## Implementation (4 tasks; A→B ordered, C/D alongside) ### Task A — Tabs primitive (`Tabs.tsx`, `Tabs.css`) - `TabDescriptor` (`Tabs.tsx:116`): add `method?: string` + `dirty?: boolean` (both optional → backward compatible; non-closable consumers omit; keep `badge`). - Method chip in both render branches, before label: `{tab.method && {tab.method}}` — uses **global** `.method`/`{METHOD}` classes (documented departure from 002's BEM-only convention). React-escaped text, safe. - Closable branch: dirty/close mutual exclusion — `tab.dirty ? : `. **Non-closable branch keeps `badge` byte-identical** (AC-11 002 guarantee). - A11y: dirty dot is a non-focusable span (zero tab stops → AC-12 roving preserved); Delete/Backspace close path is gated on `closable` not `dirty` → dirty tabs stay keyboard-closable (add test). **Accepted tradeoff:** dirty dot has no accessible name (matches reference ``); AT close routes via keyboard — document explicitly. - **AC-26 stays satisfied** — the change replaces the close *button*, never the *label* span. - CSS: replace active block with top `::before` accent + bottom `::after` mask + `--bg` bg; rewrite `.tabs__tab-close` always-visible 16px grid-centered; add `.tabs__tab-dirty` 7px `--text-faint` circle; geometry gap 8px / padding `0 10px 0 12px` / label `flex:1` ellipsis. ### Task B — TabBar (`TabBar.tsx`, `TabBar.css`) - `toDescriptor` (`:73`): add `method: tab.spec.method`, `dirty: tab.dirty`; **remove** `badge: tab.dirty ? '●' : undefined`. Leave `deriveLabel` unchanged (AC-25). Method appearing twice on unnamed-URL tabs (chip + in label) is intentional/reference-faithful. - Actions slot: `+` Icon button + `.tabbar__spacer{flex:1}` + static "More tabs" chevron button. **Overflow chevron is a static affordance in v1** (reference's is also static `

risks.risk: Shared 002 contract change; active-CSS rewrite + overflow:hidden removal could regress other Tabs consumers / clip accent; mitigated by .tabbar-scoping + byte-identical-path test + runtime probe.

plan_seeds.recommended_approach_summary: Recommended as the only candidate that completely meets the tiered acceptance bar the user defined; the cheaper alternative deliberately leaves required visuals unmet and is non-compliant by construction. It preserves every stated guarantee and REUSES the project's canonical Icon-atom idiom (glyph definitions already present in atoms/icons.ts:14) instead of reinventing it, honoring the search-first reuse rule. Residual unknowns are tracked as open questions Q1 and Q2 and settled by the live design-auditor pass rather than asserted here.

alternatives_considered.summary: Add chip span + SVG close + active-CSS + border-dedup now; rely on Shell's existing data-mstyle='soft' for chip color and DEFER the base .method{font-mono} rule, --m-head and .method.HEAD token to a follow-up.

discriminator.primary_confirms_if: If computed .method background/color is empty on a chip → tokens/data-mstyle gap confirmed (H-B); if HEAD chip uncolored while others colored → HEAD-token gap; if active ::before/::after or chevron clipped → overflow:hidden regression (H-C); all assertions pass → H-A confirmed.
